message:
style(fnp-python): apply cargo fmt to conformance test harnesses

Pure formatting-only change across seven conformance test files. No
behavioral or test-coverage changes — only line-wrapping adjustments
that bring these harnesses back into agreement with rustfmt's preferred
layout.

Files reformatted:

  - tests/conformance_array_manip.rs
  - tests/conformance_bitwise.rs
  - tests/conformance_fft.rs
  - tests/conformance_indexing.rs
  - tests/conformance_searching.rs
  - tests/conformance_setops.rs
  - tests/conformance_testing.rs

Net diff is +57 / -112: most chunks collapse manual multi-line wraps
back onto a single line where rustfmt's width budget allows it (e.g.
np_array_2d_f / np_array_3d_f / py.import("numpy").getattr(...).call1(...)
calls), and a few are the inverse — closure bodies wrapped because the
single-line form exceeded the column limit. No identifiers, arguments,
case ordering, RequirementLevel, CompareMode, or assertion logic were
touched.

This unblocks 'cargo fmt --check' as a clean gate ahead of the next
conformance harness expansion.
